title: ‘Sharam karo... Pant se kuch seekho’: Yuvraj teases Abhishek, Gill - Watch
content: Rishabh Pant gifted Yuvraj Singh a golf club and heartfelt note to thank him for mentoring him ahead of IPL 2026 after a difficult phase in his career. Sharing the gesture online, Yuvraj jokingly teased Shubman Gill, Abhishek Sharma and other mentees, saying, “Sharam karo... Rishabh Pant se kuch seekho.” Meanwhile, Pant continues to struggle for form in IPL 2026.

title: SP cuts tie-up with I-PAC: Akhilesh Yadav cites fund crunch ahead of 2027 UP polls
content: Samajwadi Party leader Akhilesh Yadav announced the party has ended its association with political consultancy I-PAC, citing financial constraints rather than electoral performance. Yadav clarified that the decision was due to limited resources, not recent election outcomes, and that the party had engaged I-PAC for early preparations for the 2027 Uttar Pradesh Assembly elections.

title: China should not have ... : Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang after share falls to zero in Beijing
content: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang declared America a priority for advanced chip sales, stating China should not have access to the "latest and greatest" AI technology. However, he urged the US not to abandon the Chinese market entirely, arguing that global sales and increased tax revenues strengthen American economic and national security.

title: Sports Ministry reiterates policy on India vs Pakistan sporting relations
content: Sports Ministry has reaffirmed its policy of a blanket ban on bilateral sporting ties with Pakistan, with no Indian teams participating in Pakistan and vice-versa. However, exemptions remain for multi-nation events like the Olympics and Asian Games. The ministry also aims to simplify visa processes to position India as a preferred international sports hosting destination.

title: As Dell CEO moves company’s base to Texas; gets message from Governor Greg Abott
content: Dell Technologies is moving its legal home from Delaware to Texas. This change is set for approval at the company's annual stockholders meeting on June 25, 2026. Texas Governor Greg Abbott welcomed the tech giant, highlighting its long history in the state.

title: IPL: Top 5 expert reactions to Sanju Samson’s match-winning 87* vs DC
content: Sanju Samson played a match-winning unbeaten knock of 87 runs. He led Chennai Super Kings to a comfortable eight-wicket victory against Delhi Capitals. Samson's composed innings, supported by Kartik Sharma, chased down the target with ease. This crucial win keeps CSK in contention for the IPL 2026 playoffs. Experts praised Samson's form and leadership.

title: Jeff Bezos, Lauren Sanchez donate millions in Fashion Grants: Where it will be used
content: Jeff Bezos and Lauren Sánchez, through the Bezos Earth Fund, have committed $34 million to research sustainable fashion materials. This funding will support universities and institutions in developing biodegradable and plastic-free alternatives to polyester and cotton, aiming to reduce the industry's environmental impact and bring these innovations to market.

title: A diary, a threat, and a $30bn stake: What Musk vs OpenAI trial has exposed
content: Seven trial days into Musk v. Altman, neither side is winning. Elon Musk filibustered his way through cross-examination. OpenAI president Greg Brockman's own diary—"what will take me to $1B?"—became the prosecution's best exhibit. A $97.4 billion bid surfaced through a passed note. And Demis Hassabis, the man who never took the stand, turns out to haunt every page of evidence.

title: Anupam Kher’s witty reply to Samik Bhattacharya’s ‘baal’ remark
content: Actor Anupam Kher humorously responded to a viral remark by West Bengal BJP chief Samik Bhattacharya, who used Kher's bald head as a metaphor for impossibility. Bhattacharya stated that the Trinamool Congress returning to power was as unlikely as hair growing back on Kher's head.

title: KSCA reacts to Bengaluru losing IPL 2026 final: ‘Specific reasons not communicated’
content: Bengaluru's M. Chinnaswamy Stadium has lost the rights to host the IPL 2026 final to Ahmedabad, much to the Karnataka Cricket Association's disappointment. Despite claiming full preparedness and a strong operational record, the KSCA was informed of the shift due to unspecified 'operational and logistical considerations' by the BCCI, sparking debate.

title: Simone Biles 'in therapy' as Jonathan Owens eyes NFL return
content: Simone Biles opens up about her mental health struggles following the Tokyo Olympics, reflecting on the pressure that led her to step away from competition. In a recent interview, the Olympic champion shared how the experience changed her perspective on strength and vulnerability. Two years later, Biles continues to focus on her well-being while keeping her future in gymnastics open, including a possible return for the 2028 Olympics in Los Angeles.

title: Samson not in peak physical condition, says Manjrekar; cites Kohli example
content: Sanjay Manjrekar urges Sanju Samson to emulate Virat Kohli's fitness dedication for sustained success. Despite Samson's stellar IPL 2026 season with two centuries and over 400 runs for CSK, Manjrekar believes the 31-year-old wicketkeeper-batter can enhance his physical conditioning. He highlighted Kohli's longevity, attributing it to prioritizing fitness to complement his skills and prevent physical limitations.

title: Video: CPM claims BJP 'goons attacked, broke Lenin statue' in Bengal's Murshidabad
content: Vladimir Lenin was a Russian revolutionary who led the 1917 Bolshevik Revolution and founded the Soviet Union, becoming one of the most influential communist leaders, having overwhelming influence in West Bengal politics under the CPM regime, which was uprooted by the TMC's Mamata Banerjee after three decades in 2011. The party said it registered a police complaint, leading to arrest of five and vowed to rebuild the statue on May 8.

title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Will one-third of the world be left in the dark?
content: Millions in Asia, including India, China, and Thailand, face uncertainty over watching the FIFA World Cup 2026 due to delayed broadcast rights. Unfavorable late-night match timings and high costs are stalling deals, potentially impacting viewership. Experts, however, believe agreements will be reached, ensuring widespread access to the global football spectacle.

title: The 2K-vote gap: How 12 narrow losses cost TVK simple majority
content: Actor Vijay's Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam narrowly missed a majority, losing 12 seats by under 2,000 votes, including one by just 285 votes. Despite these close calls, TVK secured significant victories, with some margins exceeding 60,000 votes, demonstrating a strong debut performance.

title: 'IPL lines everyone’s pockets': Alastair Cook's fresh dig at Kevin Pietersen over Jacob Bethell debate
content: Alastair Cook reignited his debate with Kevin Pietersen over Jacob Bethell's IPL stint. Cook believes the young batter needs game time in county cricket over sitting on the bench in India. Pietersen countered, asserting Cook lacks understanding of the IPL's developmental benefits. Cook reiterated his stance, emphasizing playing matches over observation, despite the IPL's financial allure.

title: 'Blasts keep happening in India, nothing new': Farooq Abdullah downplays Punjab explosions
content: Twin explosions in Punjab's Jalandhar and Amritsar have sparked political debate, with former Union minister Farooq Abdullah downplaying the incidents and Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann accusing the BJP of fear-mongering. The NIA is assisting in the investigation of the blasts, which caused property damage but no casualties.

title: Andhra Pradesh: Man feeds poison-laced sweets to wife, smothers her
content: A shocking incident unfolded in Proddatur, Andhra Pradesh, where a husband allegedly murdered his wife by poisoning her. Driven by marital discord and an extramarital affair, the man reportedly purchased a potent substance online from a YouTuber for a hefty sum.

title: 4 creative activities parents can use to help children open up emotionally
content: Therapist reveals how creative activities empower children to express difficult emotions that they can't yet verbalise. Simple, accessible methods like emotion drawing, collages and storytelling through art provide a safe outlet. These practices help children process feelings, build emotional resilience and strengthen bonds with caregivers while emphasising expression over perfection.

title: Jharkhand JAC Class 12 result 2026: Direct link to download scorecards here
content: Jharkhand Academic Council (JAC) has released the Class 12 board exam results for 2026 today, May 6. Students can check their scorecards online and on DigiLocker. A minimum of 33% is required to pass. Those unsatisfied can opt for improvement exams in theory papers. The grading system ranges from A+ to D.

title: 1.6 million-year-old bones reveal how the first humans really got their meat
content: Ancient Kenyan fossils reveal early humans employed a dynamic strategy for meat acquisition, blending hunting with opportunistic scavenging. Evidence of tool marks on bones, alongside predator tooth marks, suggests a sophisticated approach to accessing carcasses. This adaptable foraging, including marrow extraction and meat transport, was crucial for survival and brain development, moving beyond the simple hunter-scavenger debate.

title: Drunk bouncer shoots sisters-in-law point-blank after late-night argument
content: A bouncer fatally shot his two sisters-in-law in Raipur following a domestic dispute over his child. The accused, Jitendra Verma, went to his in-laws' home in an inebriated state after his wife left with their child. He surrendered to the police after the incident.

title: How Middle East turmoil opens 3 critical supply chain opportunities for India
content: India’s macro exposure to the Middle East continues to be transmitted primarily through energy and critical input prices, reflecting structurally high dependence on imported crude and natural gas.

title: ‘Hindus only’ banner at Bengal Kankalitala temple sparks row; priests claim no entry bar
content: A flex at Kankalitala temple in Bolpur declared only Hindus allowed for puja, installed by 'Sanatan Paksha'. Temple priests stated no one is prohibited from entering, but Hindus typically offer prayers. This exclusionary message contrasts with Bengal's inclusive temple traditions, as seen at Tarapith temple where no restrictions exist. Local BJP leadership denied involvement and opposed new regulations.

title: 11-year-old rape victim hangs self due to trauma, accused held
content: A 26-year-old man has been arrested for allegedly sexually assaulting an 11-year-old girl in Bareilly, who later died by suicide. The post-mortem report confirmed sexual assault and multiple injuries. The accused allegedly lured the minor and assaulted her, leading to her distress and subsequent death.

title: Why Bengaluru lost IPL 2026 final hosting rights to Ahmedabad
content: The IPL 2026 final has been moved from Bengaluru's M Chinnaswamy Stadium to Ahmedabad's Narendra Modi Stadium due to operational and logistical issues with the local association. This marks Ahmedabad's fourth final in five seasons. Previously, Ahmedabad hosted in 2022 and 2025, with scheduling and weather concerns influencing those decisions as well.

title: Punjab twin blasts: CCTV captures man running from Jalandhar site; Khalistan group claims role
content: The National Investigation Agency is investigating two recent explosions in Punjab, with teams dispatched to Jalandhar and Amritsar. These incidents, occurring within hours of each other, have raised security concerns and drawn sharp political criticism regarding the state's law and order situation.

title: At 82, MLA Sobhandeb Chattopadhyay wins a record 10th term, stands tall amid TMC defeat
content: Sobhandeb Chattopadhyay made history by winning the Ballygunge seat, becoming the only Bengal MLA to serve a 10th consecutive term. Despite his party's defeat, the 82-year-old Trinamool leader secured a significant victory, attributing his success to voter acceptance and vowing to fulfill his commitments.

title: IPL 2026: Fortress factor — how home advantage is shaping playoff race
content: IPL 2026's playoff race is heating up, with home advantage proving crucial. Top teams like Punjab Kings and Royal Challengers Bengaluru are leveraging their home grounds to secure vital points. Meanwhile, teams in the bottom half are struggling significantly at home, making their playoff aspirations increasingly difficult as the league stage concludes.

title: Pay 2X charges if you miss this payment window on barrier-less tolls
content: National Highways Authority of India warns motorists about new barrier-less tolling systems in Rajasthan and Telangana. Vehicles passing without stopping are tracked via LiDAR and FASTag. Failure to pay within 72 hours of an e-Notice results in double the original toll. Users can check and settle dues on the official portal.

title: Post-TMC rout, I-PAC's deal with Akhilesh for 2027 polls hangs in balance
content: Following the Trinamool Congress's defeat in West Bengal, the Samajwadi Party's potential pact with political consultancy firm I-PAC for the 2027 Uttar Pradesh elections is now uncertain. SP chief Akhilesh Yadav was reportedly hesitant to hire I-PAC, and the firm's recent challenges, including an ED raid, further complicate any potential deal.

title: What happens if a Chief Minister refuses to relinquish office after electoral defeat?
content: Following the Trinamool Congress’s defeat to the BJP in the West Bengal Assembly elections, Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee has announced that she will not tender her resignation. Can a Governor remove a Chief Minister? What happens once the term of the Assembly expires? What legal remedies are available to challenge the election results?

title: Andhra Pradesh SIPB clears investment proposals worth ₹2 lakh crore
content: Reliance Industries to set up a ₹1,08,010 crore data centre in Vizag and a solar power project, along with a battery energy storage system, in Sri Sathya Sai district with an outlay of ₹51,300 crore

title: Nitish Kumar’s son may join Bihar cabinet on May 7
content: Nishant Kumar who joined the JD(U) less than a couple of months ago, gave in to persuasions by senior functionaries of the party

title: Assembly elections 2026 results LIVE: New BJP govt. in Bengal expected to take oath on May 9
content: T.N. Governor Arlekar dissolves 16th State Legislative Assembly, effective May 5; CEC orders immediate arrest of those involved in post-poll violence, vandalism in Bengal

title: Plea in Madras High Court insists on FIR, money laundering probe against TVK’s Vijay over 2015 income tax dispute
content: The Registry had refused to number the case last month; however it got numbered on Wednesday (May 6, 2026) following a judicial direction to leave the question of maintainability to be decided by the judges

title: Can't be called backstabbing, what's the issue if like-minded parties forming govt in Tamil Nadu: Congress
content: The Congress, a long-time ally of the DMK, announced its support to actor-politician Vijay's TVK to form the government in Tamil Nadu and severed ties with the Dravidian major

title: Samajwadi Party has ended contract with I-PAC due to fund constraints: Akhilesh Yadav
content: He rejected the suggestion that the decision to end the deal was influenced by recent election outcomes in States such as West Bengal

title: Guntur civic body launches drive to make city poster-free; 12 enforcement teams formed
content: Guntur Municipal Commissioner K. Mayur Ashok warns of heavy fines and criminal cases against violators; printers told not to make wall posters and to carry their details on flex banners; night patrols set up to catch illegal pasting

title: Tensions escalate as bulldozer razes structures in Kolkata’s iconic New Market
content: Trinamool Congress leadership says the “poriborton” (change) promised by the BJP has arrived on a bulldozer

title: Seven panchaloha idols recovered from temple well in Palnadu; ASI to examine antiquity
content: Idols of Lord Venugopala Swamy, Rukmini Devi, Satyabhama, Lord Venkateswara Swamy, Sridevi, Bhudevi and Godadevi found submerged in a well at Sri Bhavannarayana Swamy temple in Inumella village; recovered during preliminary work for temple reconstruction

title: TVK chief Vijay calls on Governor Arlekar, stakes claim to form government in Tamil Nadu
content: The TVK has emerged as the single largest party in Tamil Nadu Assembly, winning 108 seats in the 234-member House

title: Bombay HC frees 50 seafarers stranded on 3 detained ships off Mumbai coast
content: The Bombay High Court pulled up the vessel owners, accusing them of being the least interested in human lives and only being concerned about their vessels and commercial activities

title: Supreme Court refuses to adjourn hearing on pleas against ECI law, says this matter is more important
content: A nine-judge constitution bench headed by Chief Justice Surya Kant is currently hearing petitions regarding discrimination against women at religious sites, including the Sabarimala temple in Kerala, as well as the scope of religious freedom across various faiths

title: ICC report mandatory for receiving publicity clearance certificate, says KFCC
content: The film chamber aims at ensuring proper functioning of the committees as per the provisions under the Sexual Harassment of Women at the Workplace (Prevention, Prohibition and Redressal) Act (POSH Act), 2013.

title: Telugu inscription dating back to the 16th century found at Bugga Ramalingeswara temple in Tadipatri
content: Carving on the north wall of the Anantapur district temple records obeisance paid by Basavappa, son of Satram Cheruvupalli Kalayya from Mahimaluru in Udayagiri Seema; historian MyNaa Swamy dates it to either 1574 or 1634 CE

title: Kerala elections: Social media campaigns, flexboards won’t decide who will be CM, says Ramesh Chennithala
content: It is decided by High Command in accordance with procedure in place, says senior Congress leader. Chennithala’s remarks come in response to queries on social media campaign in favour of V.D. Satheesan as Kerala CM on grounds that the latter led UDF poll campaign from the front

title: AIADMK under pressure to revisit its stand against ties with TVK
content: The tone for the reconsideration of its stance was set on Tuesday by Leema Rose, the newly elected legislator from Lalgudi, who told the media that negotiations were in progress between AIADMK general secretary Edappadi K. Palaniswami and the TVK over government formation

title: Malayali musician Aksomaniac on his latest viral track ‘Amsham’ entering Spotify Viral 50 – Global
content: ‘Amsham’ by Aksomaniac featuring MHR, Bhumi, and Circle Tone has amassed over eight million streams online, recently soaring to third place on Spotify Viral 50 – Global

title: Are people of J-K being punished for not electing a BJP CM? Omar Abdullah on statehood delay
content: “What is the yardstick to measure appropriate time? At least if we knew what the appropriate time would be, and how to measure it, we would work towards it,” J&K CM Omar Abdullah said

title: News in Frames: Waltz of the winds
content: A ‘disturbance’ in the weather system brings rain to India during non-monsoon months but can also disrupt agricultural schedules and trigger extreme events

title: BRS accuses Congress of failing farmers, threatens statewide protests
content: Four key resolutions were passed at the meeting, including a demand for financial assistance to the families of farmers who died over the past two-and-a-half years

title: Tamil Nadu election 2026: NTK candidate to plant 6,450 saplings to thank voters in Salem
content: She said the saplings would be planted in the Mookaneri area at Kannankurichi and along the lake, with the initiative aimed at increasing green cover and promoting environmental conservation.

title: Former Prasar Bharati CEO Shashi Shekhar Vempati appointed CBFC chairman
content: Shashi Shekhar Vempati has served in the UGC, television ratings agency BARC, and the Department of Science and Technology; he has been involved with the country’s AI ecosystem

title: India, Vietnam elevate ties to enhanced comprehensive strategic partnership; set $25 billion trade target
content: The two sides signed as many as 13 agreements which will provide for deeper two-way engagement in a range of areas

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: The highest and lowest margins of victory
content: Most constituencies with margins above 60,000 votes were won by the TVK, with the DMK finishing second in a majority of these seats. The distribution of these margins shows a concentration in urban and industrial regions

title: Kerala food services shutdown: LPG price hike fuels 24-hour Statewide strike, leaves thousands struggling
content: Token strike results in near-complete closure of dine-in, takeaway and online food delivery services across the State

title: Statehood will be achieved soon: Puducherry CM Rangasamy
content: Speaking to reporters in Salem, he conveyed his wishes to Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) leader C. Vijay Joseph on his political prospects

title: Stalin accorded rousing reception in Kolathur
content: The DMK president met and thanked supporters. Voters recalled his contributions to the constituency, including the construction of flyovers, community halls, and other facilities

title: Supreme Court collegium approves names of 10 advocates as judges of Punjab and Haryana High Court
content: Mr. Chauhan is currently the Advocate-General of Haryana, while Mr. Deol is Additional Advocate-General of Punjab

title: NC trying to divert attention from its 'failures': Mehbooba
content: She said the NC is welcome to fight her party politically but the ruling party should spare religious scriptures while doing so

title: Supreme Court should take cognisance of West Bengal poll process: Akhilesh Yadav
content: The former Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister alleged that the developments seen in West Bengal could be replicated on a larger scale in Uttar Pradesh, where Assembly elections are due next year

title: Suvendu Adhikari says, ‘Will vacate one of two Assembly seats won in 10 days’
content: BJP’s Suvendu Adhikari, who secured both Bhabanipur and Nandigram Assembly constituencies in the recent elections, asserted that the party's central leadership would decide which constituency he would retain

title: Ban on bilateral sports with Pakistan to continue, multinational events exempted: Ministry
content: The policy was first announced last year in August after an uproar over India's participation in cricket's Asia Cup in the UAE that also featured Pakistan

title: TVK office-bearer arrested for sexually harassing minor in Chennai
content: The accused, Dinesh, had been absconding since Monday after a sexual harassment complaint was lodged by the mother of the minor girl

title: No immediate plans for Delhi visit; leadership call up to party high command, says Siddaramaiah
content: The CM said that Congress party’s recent bypoll victories signal strong public backing for its welfare agenda, and were a clear endorsement of his government’s performance.

title: Karnataka’s ‘Adopt a Monument’ sees muted response three years after launch
content: Poor publicity, limited connectivity to several heritage locations, lack of detailed development plans, and the absence of stronger government outreach, have been pointed as some of the main reasons for the slow uptake by the stakeholders

title: Vijay’s TVK seeks CPI’s support after Congress, CPI(M) outreach
content: Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) president C. Joseph Vijay has reached out to the Communist Party of India (CPI) for support.

title: India's approach to migration grounded in dignity, respect for rights: MoS Kirti Vardhan Singh
content: He addressed a round table at the UN headquarters on the Global Compact for Safe, Orderly and Regular Migration.

title: Sringeri recounting: Karnataka Speaker says he will have to study legal and technical aspects before administring oath to D.N. Jeevaraj
content: U.T. Khader says he spoke to the Governor Thaawarchand Gehlot on Tuesday and put out his point of view

title: Kerala elections: G. Sudhakaran dismisses talks of joining Congress, blames CPI(M)’s ‘ideological drift’ for its poll rout
content: ‘I am not exploring any broader platform or planning to join any political party. I will remain an independent MLA. This allows me to raise issues freely and intervene in matters that benefit society,’ says Sudhakaran

title: Explained: Rise of BJP in West Bengal
content: For decades, West Bengal resisted the BJP’s rise but that has now changed. How did the party move from the margins to the center of power in a state shaped by Left politics and strong regional identity? Here’s a deep dive into the political shift, key turning points, and what it means going forward.

title: Kerala polls: Congress-led UDF won not due to any individual’s efforts but as people were fed up, says NSS general secretary
content: G. Sukumaran Nair’s remarks are in response to queries on Congress leader V.D. Satheesan's role in UDF victory. Says his differences with Satheesan ‘still exist’ as NSS does not like the stand of the Congress leader on some issues

title: Ashok Kharat remanded to police custody till May 9
content: The investigation seeks to determine the source of the ₹5.52 crore used for lending and for the land acquisition

title: Why industrial heat pumps are a ‘clean heat’ opportunity for India
content: Industry accounted for nearly half of India’s final energy consumption in 2025, much of it still tied to fossil fuels. Decarbonising industrial heat is therefore not just a climate issue but also one of air quality, competitiveness, energy security, and worker health

title: Vietnam President To Lam accorded ceremonial welcome at Rashtrapati Bhavan on his maiden visit to India
content: The timing of President To Lam’s trip is particularly significant as it marks the “10th anniversary of the comprehensive strategic partnership” between India and Vietnam

title: Karnataka’s deep tech programme ELEVATE NxT draws strong response from startups across India
content: Of the 983 applications received, 213 are from outside Karnataka, accounting for nearly 23% of the total

title: Odisha govt issues SOP to combat urban flooding for coming monsoon
content: Regular desilting of drains and removal of encroachments obstructing natural water flow have been made compulsory to prevent flooding and ensure smooth drainage during heavy rainfall

title: Wave of change rolls over three states, sweeps away two CMs, 50 ministers
content: Manash Pratim Gohain is a seasoned journalist with over two decades at The Times of India, where he has built a rich body of work spanning education policy, politics, and governance. Renowned for his incisive coverage of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, accreditation reforms, and skilling initiatives, he has also reported on student politics, urban policy, and social movements. His political reportage—both reflective and news-driven—adds depth to his writing, bridging policy with public impact. Through his 2,500 articles and related outlets, he has emerged as a trusted voice in national discourse, particularly in linking education reform to broader societal change. Read More

title: Bengal woman's long journey: From domestic help to assembly
content: domestic help-turned-BJP MLA says she won't eat anything today . "i had barely eaten and was hungry after a day of high tension," she says . the legislator caught up on washing clothes and other household chores after weeks of being busy with the election .

title: Over 68 lakh cyberattack attempts on counting day: EC
content: Bharti Jain is senior editor with The Times of India, New Delhi. She has been writing on security matters since 1996. Having covered the Union home ministry, security agencies, Election Commission and the ‘prime’ political beat, the Congress, for The Economic Times all these years, she moved to TOI in August 2012. Her repertoire of news stories delves into the whole gamut of issues related to terrorism and internal strife, besides probing strategic affairs in India’s neighbourhood. Read More

title: After poll result, ED changes gear, trouble seems headed TMC’s way
content: ED will question Sujit Bose and Rathin Ghosh, both ministers in the outgoing Mamata govt, in connection with its investigation into the municipality recruitment ‘scam’ case . this will be the second round of questioning of bose — fire minister defeated in his stronghold Bidhannagar — and the food minister who won from Madhyamgram . 'i will not resign', mamata alleges EC Bias, Says
